meta.title: Hello World
meta.code: print("Hello, World!")
meta.description: Your first Python program using print() function.

meta.title: Variables & Data Types
meta.code:
name = "Alice"
age = 25
height = 5.9
is_student = True

print("Name:", name)
print("Age:", age)
print("Height:", height)
print("Is Student:", is_student)
meta.final_output:
Name: Alice
Age: 25
Height: 5.9
Is Student: True
meta.description: Declare string, int, float, and boolean variables.

meta.title: Add Two Numbers
meta.code:
a = 10
b = 20
sum = a + b
print(f"Sum of {a} and {b} is: {sum}")
meta.description: Add two numbers using f-string for formatted output.

meta.title: User Input
meta.code:
name = input("Enter your name: ")
print(f"Hello, {name}!")
meta.description: Read user input and display a greeting message.

meta.title: Type Conversion
meta.code:
num_str = "42"
num_int = int(num_str)
num_float = float(num_str)

print(f"String: {num_str}")
print(f"Integer: {num_int}")
print(f"Float: {num_float}")
meta.description: Convert between string, int, and float types.

meta.title: Swap Two Numbers
meta.code:
a = 5
b = 10
a, b = b, a
print(f"After swap: a = {a}, b = {b}")
meta.description: Swap variables using Python's tuple unpacking.

meta.title: If-Else Condition
meta.description: Use if-else to make decisions based on conditions.

meta.title: Even or Odd
meta.description: Check if a number is even or odd using modulus.

meta.title: Find Maximum
meta.code:
a, b, c = 25, 18, 32
max_val = max(a, b, c)
print(f"Maximum is: {max_val}")
meta.description: Find the largest number using built-in max() function.

meta.title: For Loop Basics
meta.code:
for i in range(1, 6):
    print(i)
meta.description: Print numbers 1 to 5 using for loop with range().

meta.title: While Loop
meta.code:
count = 1

while count <= 5:
    print(f"Count: {count}")
    count += 1
meta.description: Use while loop to repeat until condition is false.

meta.title: Sum of N Numbers
meta.code:
n = 5
total = sum(range(1, n + 1))
print(f"Sum from 1 to {n} = {total}")
meta.description: Calculate sum using built-in sum() and range().

meta.title: Factorial
meta.code:
n = 5
fact = 1

for i in range(1, n + 1):
    fact *= i

print(f"Factorial of {n} = {fact}")
meta.description: Calculate factorial using a for loop.

meta.title: Star Pattern Triangle
meta.code:
for i in range(1, 6):
    print("* " * i)
meta.description: Create triangle pattern using string multiplication.

meta.title: Simple Function
meta.code:
def add(a, b):
    result = a + b
    return result

x = 5
y = 10
sum = add(x, y)
print(f"Sum = {sum}")
meta.description: Create a function that adds two numbers and returns result.

meta.title: Default Parameters
meta.code:
def greet(name="Guest"):
    print(f"Welcome, {name}!")

greet()
greet("Alice")
meta.description: Use default parameter values in functions.

meta.title: Multiple Return Values
meta.code:
def calculate(a, b):
    sum = a + b
    diff = a - b
    return sum, diff

x, y = 20, 5
add, sub = calculate(x, y)
print(f"Sum: {add}, Difference: {sub}")
meta.description: Return multiple values from a function using tuples.

meta.title: Lambda Function
meta.code:
square = lambda x: x * x

print(f"Square of 5 = {square(5)}")
print(f"Square of 7 = {square(7)}")
meta.description: Create anonymous functions using lambda expressions.

meta.title: List Basics
meta.code:
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]

for fruit in fruits:
    print(fruit)

print(f"Total fruits: {len(fruits)}")
meta.description: Create a list and iterate through its elements.

meta.title: List Operations
meta.code:
numbers = [1, 2, 3]
numbers.append(4)
numbers.insert(0, 0)
numbers.remove(2)

print(f"List: {numbers}")
print(f"First: {numbers[0]}, Last: {numbers[-1]}")
meta.description: Use append, insert, remove and indexing on lists.

meta.title: List Comprehension
meta.code:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = [x ** 2 for x in numbers]

print(f"Numbers: {numbers}")
print(f"Squares: {squares}")
meta.description: Create lists efficiently using list comprehension.

meta.title: Dictionary Basics
meta.code:
person = {
    "name": "Alice",
    "age": 25,
    "city": "New York"
}

print(f"Name: {person['name']}")
print(f"Age: {person['age']}")
print(f"City: {person['city']}")
meta.final_output:
Name: Alice
Age: 25
City: New York
meta.description: Create and access dictionary key-value pairs.

meta.title: Dictionary Loop
meta.description: Iterate through dictionary using items() method.

meta.title: String Methods
meta.code:
text = "Hello, World!"

print(f"Upper: {text.upper()}")
print(f"Lower: {text.lower()}")
print(f"Replace: {text.replace('World', 'Python')}")
print(f"Split: {text.split(', ')}")
meta.final_output:
Upper: HELLO, WORLD!
Lower: hello, world!
Replace: Hello, Python!
Split: ['Hello', 'World!']
meta.description: Use common string methods for text manipulation.

meta.title: Simple Class
meta.description: Create a class with constructor and method.

meta.title: Class Inheritance
meta.final_output:
Buddy says Woof!
Whiskers says Meow!
meta.description: Use inheritance to create specialized classes.

meta.title: Try-Except
meta.description: Handle errors gracefully using try-except blocks.

meta.title: File Write & Read
meta.code:
# Write to file
with open("test.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello, File!")

# Read from file
with open("test.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
    print(f"File content: {content}")
meta.description: Write and read files using context manager.
